characters.physical_details: Dark hair kept tied back; strong hands; scar on left wrist.

key_relationships.sev_mera: absent husband who left before the story
key_relationships.jin_parra: fellow parent carrying the same kind of lie

characters.occupation_status: Household manager and practical head of the Mera family after Sev leaves.
characters.literacy_languages: Literate; common language; recognizes her mother's southern mountain-valley lullaby dialect.

state_transitions.change: Maintains the lie that Sev went ahead to Gelden.
Confirms Asha's uncanny link to Mava and Iru.
Privately tells Jin that Sev left three months before the flood.
Names Sev's abandonment publicly and departs with Jin and Melie behind the Meras.

characters.availability: Present throughout; leaves south-southwest with the Meras, Jin, and Melie.

characters.physical_details: Lean, sharp-eyed, upright; keeps a worn blue shawl close.

key_relationships.suna_mera: daughter-in-law; critical, dependent, and more perceptive than she says
key_relationships.sev_mera: son who left before the story

characters.literacy_languages: Common language; literacy implied but not foregrounded.

state_transitions.change: Crosses the hall to comfort Mava, publicly accepting the Mera-Kondo link.
Tells Suna she will not keep dressing Sev's departure up before the final naming.
Lets Suna name Sev's abandonment without contradiction and leaves with the Meras.

characters.availability: Present throughout; leaves with the Meras.

characters.physical_details: Small, tactile, curious; always reaching for cloth, faces, and seams.

key_relationships.mava_kondo: uncanny link through Iru's word and ear gesture

characters.literacy_languages: Speaks common language; literacy not yet relevant.

state_transitions.change: Begins rubbing her left ear in Mava's gesture and saying 'Nushi' in sleep.

characters.availability: Present throughout; leaves with the Meras.

characters.physical_details: Lean, tired-eyed, slightly hunched; jaw briefly locks on hard consonants.

key_relationships.bett_dao: rescuer of Melie and practical guide
key_relationships.suna_mera: confidant who shares the same kind of lie

characters.occupation_status: Manual laborer; arrives claiming he is taking Melie to her mother's family.

state_transitions.change: Publicly claims Melie's mother died three months earlier.
Melie's anaphylaxis makes him dependent on Bett's allergy knowledge and emergency kit.
Walks out of the naming rather than continue lying with Melie present.
Tells Suna that Reva is alive and left when Melie was four.
Tells Melie the truth himself before the final naming.
Declares the truth publicly and leaves with the Meras.

characters.availability: Present throughout; leaves with Melie alongside the Meras.

characters.physical_details: Small for her age; dark hair now past her shoulders; often holds Jin's shirt in sleep.

state_transitions.change: Develops the Dao family's tree-nut allergy and survives anaphylaxis.
Learns from Jin that her mother is alive and left.
Stands through Jin's truthful declaration and leaves with him behind the Meras.

characters.availability: Present throughout; leaves with Jin alongside the Meras.

characters.physical_details: Angular, rough-handed, composed; voice carries ritual authority.

key_relationships.bram_okoye: chief skeptic she still takes seriously

characters.occupation_status: Custodian who conducts the naming; has served the shrine for twenty years.
characters.literacy_languages: Common language and shrine ritual language.

state_transitions.change: Feels the coercive ugliness of the food-for-naming bargain.
Explains the shrine's naming history to Suna and Bram and admits she cannot explain the mechanism.
Reframes the pattern as one of absences and losses rather than ancestry.
Changes the final naming's order, hears the missing names, and accepts the naming as complete.

characters.physical_details: Tall, thin, bespectacled; gestures visibly when arguing.
characters.occupation_status: Former teacher from the capital, displaced by the checkpoint regime.

state_transitions.change: Makes the political case against the naming and loses to material leverage.
Offers Jin a cigarette and silent solidarity after the failed naming.
Publicly names Efe as the child who came before Tomas.

characters.physical_details: Open-faced, visibly anxious, usually carrying Ada high against her chest.

state_transitions.change: Tells Suna that she and Bram had a son named Efe before Tomas.
Names Efe publicly for the first time at the shrine.

characters.physical_details: Lanky, braid-wearing, nail-biting, always carrying or reaching for her journal.
characters.occupation_status: Teenager and observer; nightly journal keeper.

state_transitions.change: Sees Arash and Bett together in the rain, Peyam's private prayer, and Jin's vigil; keeps it all to herself.
Records that the road is opening but the shrine's changes will travel with the families.

characters.physical_details: Round-faced and patient with younger children; later develops Jin's jaw-lock speech catch.

state_transitions.change: Begins speaking with Jin's hesitation pattern.
Hears his parents name Efe.

key_relationships.bett_dao: woman he confronts over Ori's fear

characters.occupation_status: Kondo patriarch; authority organized around keeping the family intact.

state_transitions.change: Resists the Mera-Kondo link and loses control of the room to Deshi and Mava.
Confronts Bett over Ori's terror and exposes Arash's split loyalty.
Names the family formally, then walks toward the wedding road without Arash.

characters.availability: Present throughout; leaves with eleven Kondos on the wedding road.

characters.physical_details: Lean version of Venn; conflict shows plainly on his face.

key_relationships.mava_kondo: great-aunt who reaches toward him at the final naming

characters.occupation_status: Traveling to his arranged wedding until he refuses to continue.

state_transitions.change: Begins explicit nighttime intimacy with Bett.
Refuses to back Venn's attack on Bett.
Declares he will not go to the wedding and leaves with the Daos.

characters.availability: Present throughout; leaves the Kondo party for the Daos.

characters.physical_details: Frail, white-haired; habitually rubs her left ear in slow circles.

key_relationships.iru_kondo: dead younger sister whose word and gesture return through Asha
key_relationships.asha_mera: child carrying Iru's word and ear gesture

characters.occupation_status: Elder aunt in the Kondo family.
characters.literacy_languages: Common language; retains old valley dialect from Ishe past Anke pass.

state_transitions.change: Names 'Nushi' as Iru's private word and places Iru's death on this road.
Tells the bird-nest story that briefly holds the whole hall together.

characters.availability: Present throughout; leaves with the main Kondo group.

characters.physical_details: Small, exact, white-haired; prayer-bead hands become visibly unsteady under pressure.

key_relationships.fen_tesh: chosen grandson she took from a dead family

characters.occupation_status: Elder maintaining a sixteen-year lie until chapter 14.

state_transitions.change: Knows and cooks a lentil recipe she has never made before.
Confesses Fen's origin story publicly.
Re-declares the truth without asking forgiveness.

characters.availability: Present throughout; leaves with the Teshes and Fen.

characters.physical_details: Tall, dark-haired, still; holds himself rigid when overwhelmed.

state_transitions.change: Learns he was taken as an infant from a dead roadside family.
Leaves, then returns to sit beside Gitta.
Publicly says, 'I am her grandson.'

characters.availability: Present throughout; leaves with the Teshes.

characters.physical_details: Compact, weathered, highly competent with her hands; guarded face opens rarely.

key_relationships.jin_parra: Melie's father; practical alliance through the allergy crisis

characters.occupation_status: Traveler defined by caregiving competence and long-contained grief.

state_transitions.change: Saves Melie with the Dao allergy kit, identifies the family allergy, and leaves the remaining dose with Jin.
Publicly names Ren after Venn confronts her over Ori's fear.
Names Ren again in the final naming and leaves with Arash.

characters.availability: Present throughout; leaves with the Daos and Arash.

characters.physical_details: Sturdy, practical, always half-positioned between Bett and danger.
characters.occupation_status: Dao logistics head and family shield.

state_transitions.change: Helps translate the anti-naming debate into practical child-protection rules.
Moves physically between Bett and Venn.
Argues that the changed declarations cannot honestly stay the same.

characters.availability: Present throughout; leaves with the Dao family and Arash.

characters.physical_details: Careful, watchful, visibly protective around Pik.
characters.occupation_status: Traveler navigating hostile family paperwork and protecting Pik from scrutiny.

state_transitions.change: Declares Wynn as partner and Pik as son under the shrine's traditional categories.
Raises Pik's unclear institutional records and asks whether the naming can unsettle an adopted child's place.
Declares Pik as son without amendment or apology.

characters.availability: Present throughout; leaves with Wynn, Vera, and Pik.

timeline.story_start: Late monsoon during a political crackdown; seven families are stranded at a hilltop shrine above a flooded seven-road convergence.
timeline.estimated_span: 21 days; rain stops on Day 19, road-clearing news arrives on Day 20, and the road opens on Day 21.

seasonal_track.season: Late monsoon: constant humidity, repeated rain, and a flood holding the roads below.
Monsoon break: stars return, water recedes, and the road gradually reappears.

key_events.event: Families arrive at the shrine and receive sleeping assignments.
First naming fixes each family's public story.
Daily work routines and the mixed-age children's play group form.
Asha manifests Mava's ear gesture and Iru's word 'Nushi.'
Second naming forces the Mera-Kondo connection into public view.
Melie suffers anaphylaxis from cashew paste; Bett uses the Dao emergency kit.
Bram challenges the naming; Olet states that food access is tied to compliance.
Tomas develops Jin's speech pattern, and Kiri hums Suna's dead mother's lullaby.
Communal cooking produces the Deshi-Hala bread echo and Gitta's unknown recipe.
Fasa's questioning cracks Jin's death story; he walks out rather than lie before Melie.
Leni sees Arash and Bett together; Peyam prays alone; distant violence carries from the lowlands.
Fasa tells Suna and Bram the shrine's history; Olet says the road should already be open; Pik begins Gitta's prayer-thumb gesture.
Ori develops Ren's water terror; Bett publicly names Ren after Venn's confrontation.
Gitta confesses Fen's true origin; Fen later returns to sit beside her.
Fasa concludes the pattern tracks losses and absences more than ancestry.
Rain stops; stars return; Suna and Jin privately confess the truth about their absent spouses.
The radio reports road crews clearing the pass; the families argue over one final naming.
Final naming, road reopening, and departure; Jin joins the Meras and Arash leaves with the Daos.

geography.primary_setting: A hilltop transit shrine above a flooded seven-road convergence in a monsoon mountain region.

key_locations.name: Main hall
key_locations.details: Stone hall open to the valley; site of the naming, communal gatherings, and much of the children's play.

key_locations.name: Sleeping quarters
key_locations.details: Cramped mats and thin partitions off the main hall; sound carries easily.

key_locations.name: Kitchen overhang
key_locations.details: Covered outdoor cooking area with communal fire, pots, and rationed stores.

key_locations.name: Prayer courtyard
key_locations.details: Open stone courtyard for shrine prayers, private talks, night meetings, and Suna and Jin's chapter 16 confessions.

key_locations.name: Cistern
key_locations.details: Spring-fed stone basin for washing, water collection, and Fasa's private thinking.

key_locations.name: Terraced garden
key_locations.details: Herb and vegetable terraces below the compound; also where Fen withdraws after Gitta's confession.

key_locations.name: Custodians' quarters
key_locations.details: Restricted side building holding the radio and the custodians' work space.

key_locations.name: Switchback road and convergence
key_locations.details: Mud descent to the valley and seven-road junction; submerged most of the book, visible in chapter 17, passable in chapter 18.

distances: The switchback descent from shrine to valley takes about fifteen minutes when dry.
The flood covers the lower switchbacks and the seven-road convergence until chapter 18.
A gauging stone stands at the third bend and marks abnormal flood height.
Kitchen, hall, courtyard, cistern, and sleeping areas are all within a short walk, so almost nothing stays unheard.

world_rules: Families staying more than one night must submit to the naming to eat from common stores.
Five custodians run the shrine: Olet, Fasa, and three assistants.
No phone service; the custodians' radio is the only outside-news link.
The lowland crisis means checkpoints, identity scrutiny, curfews, and delayed road work.
The custodians can repeat the naming until they judge it complete.
Manifestations cross family lines through gestures, vocabulary, allergies, speech patterns, songs, fears, prayer habits, and one adult craft memory; the mechanism stays unconfirmed.
Fasa's chapter 15 reading is that the pattern tracks losses and absences more than ancestry.
Olet believes the road reopens only when the naming is complete.

power_structure.mechanism: Controls rooms, schedule, radio access, and the food stores tied to naming compliance.
Ritual authority, questioning, and the power to keep a declaration open.
Patriarchal habit, travel authority, and control of the wedding plan, weakened after Ori's fear and Arash's refusal.
Protective authority, kitchen competence, and child-placement decisions.

power_structure.over: daily survival for the Mera family
power_structure.mechanism: Competence, labor, and the trust others place in her practical judgment.

objects.name: Sev's folded note
objects.origin: Left behind when Sev Mera abandoned the family
objects.status: Carried in Suna's bag through the shrine stay as proof of Sev's departure.

objects.name: Custodians' radio
objects.status: Only outside-news link; carries checkpoint, violence, and road-clearing reports.

objects.name: Leni's journal
objects.status: Written in nightly; records the shrine's changing facts and private observations.

objects.name: Deshi's blue shawl
objects.status: Worn constantly through the shrine stay; the personal item Deshi keeps closest.

objects.name: Bett's allergy tin
objects.status: Contains the epinephrine used on Melie in chapter 6; Bett leaves the remaining dose with Jin.

objects.name: Peyam's pipe
objects.origin: Personal possession wrapped in cloth in his pack
objects.status: Shared with Kal in chapter 9 and packed again for departure.

knowledge_state.knows: Sev left the family three months before chapter 1; he neither went ahead nor died.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Hidden from the children and wider shrine until chapter_18.

knowledge_state.knows: Reva is alive and left him and Melie when Melie was four.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Hidden from the shrine through chapter_16 and from Melie until chapter_17.

knowledge_state.knows: Fen is not blood kin; Gitta found him alive beside a dead roadside family sixteen years earlier.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Hidden from Miro, Enn, Fen, and everyone else until chapter_14.

knowledge_state.knows: She and Bram had a son named Efe before Tomas; he lived three months and died.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Known to Bram; hidden from the children and most families until chapter_18.

knowledge_state.knows: Ren drowned at age three after weeks of inexplicable fear of water.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Known inside the Dao family; hidden from the wider shrine until chapter_13.

knowledge_state.knows: Pik's institutional records are incomplete and the Leo family cannot verify his birth family.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Held inside the Leo family until Cade raises it openly in chapter_17.

knowledge_state.knows: Arash Kondo and Bett Dao have a secret nighttime relationship.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Hidden from the adults; Leni never tells anyone.

knowledge_state.knows: He believes the road reopens only when the naming is complete.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Hidden from the families until Fasa relays it to Suna and Bram in chapter_12.

knowledge_state.knows: Fasa concludes the manifestations map absences and losses more than ancestry.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Shared with Olet, but not directly with the families as a finished theory.

knowledge_state.knows: Her mother is alive and left; this replaces the death story before the final naming.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Only partly legible to the wider shrine before chapter_18.

knowledge_state.knows: Reva Parra is alive and left Jin and Melie when Melie was four.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Hidden from the wider shrine until Jin speaks in chapter_18; Melie learns first in chapter_17.

knowledge_state.knows: Sev Mera left Suna and the children three months before the flood.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Hidden from the Mera children and the wider shrine until Suna speaks in chapter_18.

knowledge_state.knows: Pik has begun Gitta Tesh's prayer-thumb pattern before the final naming.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Held inside the Leo family until chapter_17 and chapter_18.

environmental_constants: The flood is always audible below the shrine and grows louder after rain.
Stone dominates every space: cold at night, warm in sun, slick in rain, and bad at keeping sound private.
Cooking smoke and damp cloth cling to people, mats, and bags after the first few days.
Oil lamps, moths, and moving shadows define evenings until the sky clears.
By chapters 16-18 open stars, birds, and drying flagstones replace the rain.
